deltas.releaseNoteText: REST API Backend: The sdk.api.* namespace is now available, introducing a REST API backend for the Python SDK with feature parity with the Velt Node SDK.
deltas.targetSection: Install and Initialize the Velt Python SDK
deltas.summary: Replace class-based VeltSdk/VeltSdkConfig init pattern with the new dict-based VeltSDK.initialize({}) pattern; MongoDB config is now optional (only required for sdk.selfHosting.*); sdk.api.* works with only apiKey and authToken

parameters.name: config
parameters.description: Dict with apiKey (str), authToken (str), and optional database/aws/collections/user_schema keys for self-hosting

deltas.releaseNoteText: Environment Variable Support: The SDK reads VELT_API_KEY, VELT_AUTH_TOKEN, VELT_WORKSPACE_ID, and VELT_WORKSPACE_AUTH_TOKEN from the environment for credential configuration.
deltas.targetSection: Install and Initialize the Velt Python SDK
deltas.summary: Document the four supported environment variables (VELT_API_KEY, VELT_AUTH_TOKEN, VELT_WORKSPACE_ID, VELT_WORKSPACE_AUTH_TOKEN) as an alternative to passing credentials in the config dict

parameters.name: VELT_WORKSPACE_ID
parameters.description: Default workspace ID for workspace-scoped operations; no config dict equivalent

parameters.name: VELT_WORKSPACE_AUTH_TOKEN
parameters.description: Auth token scoped to a specific workspace; no config dict equivalent

details.codeExample:
# Set env vars instead of hardcoding in config dict:
# VELT_API_KEY=your_key
# VELT_AUTH_TOKEN=your_token

from velt_py import VeltSDK

# Config dict can be empty when env vars are set
sdk = VeltSDK.initialize({})

deltas.releaseNoteText: REST API Services: 17 services are included: organizations, folders, documents, users, userGroups, notifications, commentAnnotations, activities, accessControl, crdt, presence, livestate, recordings, rewriter, gdpr, workspace, and token.
deltas.summary: Create a new rule documenting the sdk.api.* REST namespace: how to access the 17 services, the typed dataclass request pattern, camelCase method naming convention, and the response format (result key on success, error key on failure)

details.returnType: dict with 'result' key on success or 'error' key on failure
details.codeExample:
from velt_py import VeltSDK
from velt_py.models.organization import AddOrganizationsRequest, GetOrganizationsRequest
from velt_py.models.document import AddDocumentsRequest

sdk = VeltSDK.initialize({
    'apiKey': 'YOUR_VELT_API_KEY',
    'authToken': 'YOUR_VELT_AUTH_TOKEN'
})

# Organizations service
result = sdk.api.organizations.addOrganizations(
    AddOrganizationsRequest(
        organizations=[{'organizationId': 'org-123', 'organizationName': 'My Org'}]
    )
)

# Documents service
result = sdk.api.documents.addDocuments(
    AddDocumentsRequest(
        organizationId='org-123',
        documents=[{'documentId': 'doc-1', 'documentName': 'My Doc'}]
    )
)

# Check result
if 'error' in result:
    print('Failed:', result['error'])
else:
    print('Success:', result['result'])

# Available services under sdk.api.*:
# organizations, folders, documents, users, userGroups,
# notifications, commentAnnotations, activities, accessControl,
# crdt, presence, livestate, recordings, gdpr, workspace, token
# (rewriter excluded per sync policy)

deltas.releaseNoteText: Custom Error Classes: Three new error classes are available: VeltValidationError, VeltTokenError, and VeltApiError, all extending the base VeltSDKError.
deltas.summary: Add the three new Python exception classes (VeltValidationError, VeltTokenError, VeltApiError extending VeltSDKError) to the error handling section alongside the existing error-code response format
deltas.ambiguityNote: Release notes confirm the three classes and base class name. Import path (velt_py.exceptions) is inferred from package naming conventions — exact module path not stated in release notes. Agent-2 should verify against the python.mdx source before finalizing the import path in any code example.
